_comment: SI prefix table for the RESCUED parameter path ONLY (storeRawValue).
A value arriving under a column name no dictionary recognises used to be stored with its
number read but NEVER scaled — `"80m\u03a9@10V"` became 80 instead of 0.08, a thousand times
too high, in a slot the matching engine scores on (weight 9, `lte`). Measured over all 429
source files: 153,993 values were stored at the wrong scale, 50,521 of them in scoring slots,
WHY AN ALLOWLIST RATHER THAN A RULE. A prefix rule that keys off the first letter is what
caused the damage it is supposed to fix: `startsWith('p')` reads `ppm` as pico. Measured over
the corpus, a first-letter rule mis-converts 11,254 `pcs`, 8,132 `ppm/`, 2,990 `ppm`, plus
`mil` / `Max` / `To` / `KE` / `KP`. But a strict whole-token rule is not the answer either --
There are 367 distinct unit spellings in this corpus; recognising them all is its own project.
SCOPE: the rescued path only. The dictionary-mapped paths already normalise and are NOT
touched -- applying this table there would REMOVE conversions that work today.
Shared verbatim by lib/services/atlasMapper.ts and scripts/atlas-ingest.mjs (the live ingest
path, which cannot import TS). One file, not two hand-copied lists -- see [[two-lists-will-drift]].
_safetyComment: ⚠️ THE ALLOWLIST IS NOT SELF-VALIDATING. An earlier version of this file claimed the
conversion 'can only make a number more correct or leave it untouched -- it can never
introduce a wrong one'. THAT WAS FALSE, and the counterexample was in the corpus the whole
time: the cross generates `Gs`, which here means GAUSS (magnetic flux density), not
giga-second. 229 real values on Hall-effect sensors were being multiplied by 1e9.
The lesson is about the AUDIT, not the table. The original exclusion list guarded `MS`/`KS`,
which occur ZERO times in the corpus, while the collision that actually occurs 229 times was
never checked -- hypothetical collisions were reasoned about, real ones were not measured.
So: any change to `prefixes` or `atoms` MUST be followed by enumerating every generated token
that ACTUALLY OCCURS in data/atlas/*.json, with counts, and reading the list. That audit is
pinned as a test (atlasRescueUnits.test.ts, 'every allowlisted token that occurs in the
corpus is accounted for'). Adding an atom widens the cross by 9 tokens; adding a prefix
widens it by 11. Neither is a one-line change.
_prefixComment: Stored as EXPONENTS, not multipliers, so a negative power can DIVIDE instead of multiply:
`9 * 1e-3` is 0.009000000000000001 but `9 / 1000` is exactly 0.009. Measured over the corpus,
that difference is not cosmetic -- 69,351 of 339,725 conversions (20.4%) were not bit-exact
under multiply versus 8,454 (2.5%) under divide. It matters because evaluateThreshold
(matchingEngine.ts) compares with bare >= / <= and NO epsilon, unlike evaluateIdentity which
has a 1e-6 relative tolerance: a source `rds_on` of '0.009 \u03a9' against a candidate '9m\u03a9'
-- the same resistance -- hard-FAILED on a 1.73e-18 artifact, and passed if the two sides
GREEK MU (U+03BC) IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT and its absence is NOT an oversight.
extractNumericWithPrefix's unit character class is [a-zA-Z\u00b5\u03a9\u00b0%/\u221a] -- it
accepts the MICRO SIGN (U+00B5) but not GREEK SMALL LETTER MU (U+03BC). So a value written
with U+03BC yields NO parsed unit at all and can never reach this table; listing it here
would be dead code that reads like working code. (`applyUnitPrefix` already has a U+03BC
branch that is unreachable for exactly this reason -- a pre-existing gap, logged in BACKLOG.
Widening that regex would change the dictionary-MAPPED paths too, which is out of scope here.)
_excludedComment: Combinations the cross DOES produce but which are NOT prefixes in this corpus.
Gs / GS  -- GAUSS, 229 real values (135 + 94) on Hall-effect switches and magnetic sensors
Ms / Ks  -- a mis-cased millisecond/kilosecond. 0 in the corpus today, but mis-casing is
demonstrably real here (`KHz` 1,511x, `K\u03a9` 1,994x -- which is why uppercase K
is a prefix at all), so the next supplier drop is a live risk.
MS / KS  -- same ambiguity, uppercase-S spelling.
MH / Mh  -- mega-henry is not a real component value; the corpus `"1MH"` is a MHz typo.
⚠️ CASE MATTERS AND THE LOWERCASE SIBLINGS MUST SURVIVE. Excluding `MH` must not touch `mH`
(millihenry, 133 real values); excluding `Ms` must not touch `ms`; `mS`, `nS`, `ks`, `G\u03a9`
(689), `GHz` (359) all keep converting. Each is pinned by a test.
`mm` is NOT listed: `m` is not an atom, so the cross never generates it, and an exclusion
that cannot fire is a lie about the mechanism. Millimetre is safe because this is an
ALLOWLIST -- anything absent is left alone. A test pins that.
Uppercase P / U / N are deliberately ABSENT. A supplier writing `PF` for picofarad is real
(1,989 values), but enabling case-folding generatively also enables `UV`, `PW` and `NA` on
tokens that mean something else. That is a separate, measured change -- not this one.
